message:
fix(pool): wake waiting acquirers when warmup-created resources become idle

commit_create_slot_as_idle() adds a newly created resource directly to
the idle queue (used during pool warmup), but it never woke any pending
acquirers. If a caller was blocked in acquire() waiting for a resource,
warmup-created resources sat in the idle queue invisibly until some
other event (timeout, unrelated release) happened to wake the waiter.

Now pops the front waiter from the waiters queue and wakes it after
adding the resource to idle, matching the behavior of the normal
release() path. The lock is dropped before calling wake() to avoid
holding the pool state mutex during the wake syscall.

New integration test (tests/pool_warmup_wakeup.rs) verifies that a
waiter blocked on acquire() is woken when a warmup resource completes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
